text:
Client Side Dashboard Blueprint
    Here is a concise developer blueprint for the client-side dashboard (PSA + automation
                  console), so an engineer can go straight to implementation.[1][2]




High-level goals

 ‚óè‚Äã Single ‚ÄúCommand Center‚Äù for clients to see project, financial, and automation health in
     one place.[1]

 ‚óè‚Äã Role-based variants for Executive, Finance, Operations, IT with the same layout but
     different metrics and filters.[2][1]

 ‚óè‚Äã Real-time or near-real-time view of automation status, savings, and exceptions, integrated
     with PSA data.[2]




Layout & information architecture

Top-level layout (desktop, ~1200px wide):[1]

 ‚óè‚Äã Header (top bar)

       o‚Äã Left: logo, workspace selector, environment badge (Prod/Sandbox).

       o‚Äã Center: global date range selector (Today / 7d / 30d / Custom).

       o‚Äã Right: role switcher (Exec / Ops / Finance / IT), notifications bell, user menu.

 ‚óè‚Äã Main content area

       o‚Äã Row 1 ‚Äì KPI strip (4‚Äì6 cards)

             ‚ñ™‚Äã   Examples:


                     ‚ô£‚Äã Cost savings YTD


                     ‚ô£‚Äã Automation rate (overall %)
                 ‚ô£‚Äã Bot uptime (last 7 days)


                ‚ô£‚Äã FTE hours freed


                ‚ô£‚Äã Revenue this month (from PSA)


                ‚ô£‚Äã Compliance score (security/regulatory)


     o‚Äã Row 2 ‚Äì Trend & comparison charts

          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Cost savings over time (line chart).


          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Processes automated by month (bar).


          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Error rate trend (line with threshold).


          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Before/after cycle time comparison (bar).


     o‚Äã Row 3 ‚Äì Operational tables

          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Active automations / bots table (status, uptime, errors, owner).


          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Process metrics table (volume, cycle time, success rate, savings).


     o‚Äã Row 4 ‚Äì Alerts & compliance

          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Alerts feed (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW, filterable).


          ‚ñ™‚Äã   Compliance/audit widget (score, certifications, recent audit events).


‚óè‚Äã Side navigation (left)

     o‚Äã Dashboard (default)

     o‚Äã Automations

     o‚Äã Processes

     o‚Äã Projects & Billing
        o‚Äã Compliance & Audit

       o‚Äã Settings

Mobile: 1-column stack, KPI cards in horizontal carousel; tables become stacked cards.[2][1]




Core components & props (React)

Target stack: React + TypeScript + Tailwind + Recharts + Axios.[1][2]

 ‚óè‚Äã <DashboardLayout>

       o‚Äã Props: userRole, tenantId.

       o‚Äã Renders header, sidebar, and child routes; provides context (role, date range).

 ‚óè‚Äã <DashboardHeader>

       o‚Äã Props: role, onRoleChange, dateRange, onDateRangeChange.

       o‚Äã Contains logo, workspace dropdown, role selector, notifications, user menu.

 ‚óè‚Äã <KpiCardGrid>

       o‚Äã Props: metrics: KpiMetric[], loading.

       o‚Äã KpiMetric: { id, label, value, delta, trend, unit, severity }.

       o‚Äã Shows 4‚Äì6 cards; card color varies by severity (success/warn/error).

 ‚óè‚Äã <TrendChartsSection>

       o‚Äã Props:

            ‚ñ™‚Äã   costSavingsSeries (array of {date, value}).



            ‚ñ™‚Äã   automationRateSeries.



            ‚ñ™‚Äã   errorRateSeries.



            ‚ñ™‚Äã   beforeAfterSeries (per-process pair).


       o‚Äã Uses Recharts LineChart/BarChart; all charts share date-range filter.
  ‚óè‚Äã <BotStatusTable>

       o‚Äã Props: bots: BotRow[], onRowClick(botId).

       o‚Äã Columns: Name, Process, Status, Uptime %, Last Run, Error Count.

       o‚Äã Supports sort, filter by status; expandable row to show last runs.

 ‚óè‚Äã <ProcessMetricsTable>

       o‚Äã Props: processes: ProcessMetricRow[].

       o‚Äã Columns: Process, Volume, Avg Cycle Time, Success %, Savings YTD, Owner.

 ‚óè‚Äã <AlertsFeed>

       o‚Äã Props: alerts: Alert[], onFilterChange(severity|source).

       o‚Äã Shows time, severity, title, description, related process/bot; filter chip bar.

 ‚óè‚Äã <CompliancePanel>

       o‚Äã Props: score, areas, certifications, recentEvents.

       o‚Äã Circular progress for overall score; list of areas (e.g., HIPAA, SOX), each with
          sub-score.

 ‚óè‚Äã <RoleSwitch>

       o‚Äã Props: role, rolesAvailable, onChange.

       o‚Äã Changes which metrics & columns are visible by applying a simple mapping (e.g.,
          Exec hides low-level logs).[1]




Data contracts & API endpoints

Base: /api/dashboard/:tenantId (or /tenants/:tenantId/dashboard in BFF).[1]

 ‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/summary

       o‚Äã Returns KPI metrics and global stats, shaped for <KpiCardGrid>.

       o‚Äã Response sample:
          ‚ñ™‚Äã   totalSavingsYtd



         ‚ñ™‚Äã   automationRate



         ‚ñ™‚Äã   botUptime7d



         ‚ñ™‚Äã   fteHoursFreed



         ‚ñ™‚Äã   revenueThisMonth



         ‚ñ™‚Äã   complianceScore


‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/trends?from=&to=

    o‚Äã Cost savings series.

    o‚Äã Automation rate series.

    o‚Äã Error rate series.

    o‚Äã Before/after comparisons per process.

‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/bots?status=

    o‚Äã Bot list for <BotStatusTable>.

    o‚Äã Each row: { id, name, processName, status, uptimePct, lastRunAt, lastErrorAt,
        errorCount7d }.

‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/processes

    o‚Äã Process metrics for <ProcessMetricsTable>.

    o‚Äã Row: { id, name, volume30d, avgCycleTime, successRate, savingsYtd, owner }.

‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/alerts?severity=&limit=

    o‚Äã Alerts feed for <AlertsFeed>.

‚óè‚Äã GET /dashboard/compliance

    o‚Äã Score and audit snippets for <CompliancePanel>.
 Optional: WebSocket or SSE at /dashboard/stream for live alerts and bot status updates (5‚Äì30
second refresh).[2]




State management & behavior

 ‚óè‚Äã Use React Query (or SWR) for data fetching + caching; key off tenantId, role, dateRange.

 ‚óè‚Äã Derived role views: apply mapping to which metrics/columns show; avoid separate
     endpoints if possible.

 ‚óè‚Äã Auto-refresh:

       o‚Äã KPIs/charts: refresh every 60‚Äì300 seconds.

       o‚Äã Alerts/bot status: every 5‚Äì15 seconds or via WebSocket push.

 ‚óè‚Äã Errors: show banner + per-widget error states (empty/error placeholders).




RBAC and security considerations

 ‚óè‚Äã Frontend gets a signed JWT or opaque token with claims: tenantId, roles, scopes.

 ‚óè‚Äã All dashboard calls must include tenant and are validated server-side; no role logic trusted
     solely in frontend.

 ‚óè‚Äã Per-component guards: hide or disable sections user is not allowed to see (e.g., finance
     cannot view IT logs).




Visual design tokens (summary)

From existing design system for site and dashboard.[1]

 ‚óè‚Äã Colors:

       o‚Äã Primary: #38bdf8 (teal).

       o‚Äã Background: #0f172a (dark), surface: #111827 / #1f2933.

       o‚Äã Success: #22c55e, Warning: #f59e0b, Error: #dc2626.
  ‚óè‚Äã Typography: system sans (-apple-system, Segoe UI), 16px body, 24‚Äì32px for key KPI
     numbers.

 ‚óè‚Äã Spacing: 4px grid, 16px card padding, 24px section spacing.

 ‚óè‚Äã Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA; ensure chart colors have sufficient contrast and include
     labels/tooltips.




Implementation phases (client dashboard only)

 ‚óè‚Äã Week 1‚Äì2: Layout + static components (header, sidebar, cards, tables, charts with mock
     data).

 ‚óè‚Äã Week 3‚Äì4: Wire to real APIs / BFF, implement role-based filtering and date-range
     handling.

 ‚óè‚Äã Week 5‚Äì6: Add WebSocket/SSE for alerts, optimize performance, do accessibility and
     responsive QA.

If you want, the next step can be a very concrete artifact: e.g., a routes/dashboard.tsx file
structure with component imports, or a JSON schema for the /dashboard/summary endpoint.
